judgments.comparative_verdict: Both are strong and comparable on three expectations, but Report 2 edges ahead by explicitly rejecting deferral and endorsing same-commit doc updates going forward (expectation c), which Report 1 omits.

report_1.evidence: New refund calls should live in `backend/app/billing/gateway.py` ... `backend/app/api/invoices.py:14-16` ... `backend/app/workers/dunning.py:9-13` ... The only other provider import is `backend/app/billing/errors.py:6`, which imports just `chargekit.errors` exception classes — that is the boundary exception the doc explicitly permits, so it is not a deviation.

report_2.evidence: New refund calls should be a thin accessor (e.g. `refund(...)`) in `medibill/backend/app/billing/gateway.py` ... `medibill/backend/app/api/invoices.py:4,14-16` ... `medibill/backend/app/workers/dunning.py:2,9-13` ... the sanctioned `errors.py` exception ... (it imports only the SDK's error classes, `errors.py:6-9`, which matches its documented role)

report_1.evidence: `InvoiceOut` (`backend/app/models/invoice.py:16-22`) has a sixth field, `external_ref`, which `charge_invoice` populates and returns

report_2.evidence: The actual serializer `InvoiceOut` (`backend/app/models/invoice.py:16-22`) has a sixth field, `external_ref`, which is populated by the charge route (`invoices.py:17`) and backed by migration `backend/migrations/0006_add_invoice_external_ref.sql`

drift between it and the code is a bug to fix now — not after the refund feature lands ... before (or as the first commit of) the refund work ... Going forward, any change the refund feature makes to what the doc governs ... must update `ARCHITECTURE.md` in the same commit and be called out in that commit/PR summary.

report_1.evidence: hard-coding an exact count ("4 webhook event types") guarantees the doc rots on every addition. Better to enumerate the types without a count and point at `backend/app/api/webhooks.py` as the source of truth

report_2.evidence: handles **five**: those four plus `payment_pending`. So both the count and the list are stale ... a hard-coded count in prose is guaranteed to drift again. Drop the number entirely and either point readers at `backend/app/api/webhooks.py` as the source of truth ... or keep the list without asserting an exact count
